 {"id":"wa-upg.7","title":"[EPIC] Prepare/commit approvals with plan-hash binding (TOCTOU-safe)","description":"# [EPIC] Prepare/commit approvals with plan-hash binding

## Mission
Make approvals safer and more ergonomic by splitting unsafe actions into:
- **prepare**: compute and display the ActionPlan + deterministic hash
- **commit**: execute exactly that plan only if the user approved the hash

This prevents a common failure mode:
- user approves "something" but the action changes before execution (TOCTOU)

## Why This Matters
Approval UX is a trust boundary. We want:
- explicit, reviewable intent
- low confusion
- auditability
- minimal chance of approving the wrong thing

## Scope
- Extend allow-once approvals to bind to a **plan hash**.
- Add CLI flows:
  - `wa prepare ...` (or `--dry-run --prepare`)
  - `wa commit <hash>`
- Robot/MCP flows return:
  - plan
  - plan_hash
  - approval instructions

## Success Criteria
- Users approve a plan hash, not a vague action.
- Commits are rejected if the plan differs or has expired.
- Audit trail records prepare/approve/commit chain.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - plan hash stability
  - approval binding correctness
  - rejection on mismatch
- E2E:
  - prepare → approve → commit succeeds
  - prepare → plan changes → commit denied with actionable reason

## Acceptance Criteria
- Prepare/commit flow exists for at least SendText and workflow execution.
- Approvals are scoped, expiring, and auditable.

 {"id":"wa-upg.7.1","title":"Design: prepare/commit UX + plan-hash binding semantics","description":"# Task: Design prepare/commit approvals

## Goal
Specify the prepare/commit UX and the exact semantics of plan-hash binding.

## Requirements
- UX flows:
  - human CLI: prepare shows plan + hash, commit executes
  - robot: returns structured prepare result with remediation
  - MCP: same contract as robot
- Binding semantics:
  - approvals bind to:
    - workspace
    - plan_hash
    - action kind(s)
    - target pane_uuid (when applicable)
    - TTL
  - commit must refuse execution if:
    - plan hash mismatch
    - TTL expired
    - target pane identity changed
- Errors:
  - stable error codes and actionable remediation

## Acceptance Criteria
- The design prevents TOCTOU and confused-deputy approval mistakes.

 {"id":"wa-upg.7.2","title":"Implement wa prepare/commit commands (plan preview + hash-bound execution)","description":"# Task: Implement `wa prepare` / `wa commit`

## Goal
Implement prepare/commit commands (or flags) that expose ActionPlans and execute them only when approved.

## Requirements
- `wa prepare <action>`:
  - computes ActionPlan
  - prints plan and hash
  - returns machine output via `--format json`
- `wa commit <plan_hash>`:
  - looks up prepared plan (or accepts plan payload)
  - checks approval binding
  - executes plan with durable step logs

## Testing
- CLI contract tests for JSON output.
- Unit tests for reject cases (unknown hash, expired, mismatch).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Prepare output is stable and usable.
- Commit refuses unsafe/mismatched execution with actionable errors.

-{"id":"wa-upg.7.3","title":"Extend allow-once approvals to bind to plan_hash + plan summary","description":"# Task: Plan-hash bound approvals

## Goal
Extend approval tokens so they can securely authorize a specific ActionPlan.

## Requirements
- Approval record includes:
  - plan_hash
  - plan_version
  - scope (workspace, pane_uuid)
  - TTL
  - risk summary
- Approval lookup validates all bindings before allowing commit.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - mismatch is rejected
  - expired is rejected
  - scope violations are rejected

## Acceptance Criteria
- Approvals are unforgeable for "different plan" scenarios.

 {"id":"wa-upg.7.4","title":"Audit chain: record prepare→approve→commit with correlation IDs","description":"# Task: Audit prepare/approve/commit chain

## Goal
Make approvals auditable and explainable by recording the full chain.

## Requirements
- Record events:
  - prepare created (plan_hash, summary)
  - approval granted (who, TTL, scope)
  - commit executed (success/failure, step logs)
- Correlation:
  - all entries share a correlation id
- Redaction:
  - never store secrets in plan or audit

## Testing
- Integration tests validate audit entries and redaction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a plan_hash, users can trace the full approval/execution history.

 {"id":"wa-upg.7.5","title":"Tests: prepare/commit hash binding + mismatch rejection","description":"# Task: Tests for prepare/commit approvals

## Goal
Ensure prepare/commit approvals are correct and hard to misuse.

## Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - plan hash stability
  - commit rejects when plan differs
  - commit rejects when TTL expired
  - commit rejects when scope mismatched (workspace/pane_uuid)
- Output tests:
  - stable error codes and remediation text

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests would catch any regression that allows committing an unapproved plan.

 {"id":"wa-upg.7.6","title":"E2E: prepare→approve→commit succeeds; mismatch commit denied","description":"# Task: E2E prepare/commit approvals

## Goal
Prove prepare/commit approvals work end-to-end and prevent TOCTOU errors.

## Scenario A (happy path)
- Run `wa prepare ...` for a gated action.
- Approve the plan hash.
- Run `wa commit <hash>` and verify success.

## Scenario B (mismatch)
- Prepare a plan.
- Change the underlying inputs so the recomputed plan differs.
- Attempt commit and verify:
  - denied
  - actionable reason (hash mismatch)

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include:
  - prepare JSON
  - approval grant record
  - commit result + step logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E demonstrates both correctness and safety properties.
